2605.02050.title: Principles and Guidelines for Randomized Controlled Trials in AI Evaluation
2605.02050.paper_summary: This work establishes a foundational framework for standardizing AI evaluation RCTs (sometimes called human uplift studies). Drawing on established experimental practices from disciplines with established RCT traditions, including software engineering, economics, clinical and health sciences, and psychology, we adopt the (Shadish et al., 2002) four-validity framework and extend it with a fifth principle on transparency, repeatability, and verification adapted from the Transparency and Openness Promotion (TOP) Guidelines (Center for Open Science, 2025). We operationalize all five principles into 33 guidelines adapted for AI evaluation RCT contexts, expressed as requirements with rationales, implementation instructions, and evidence bases. We position the principles and guidelines as serving three key roles for AI evaluation RCTs: a design tool for planning studies, an evaluation rubric for assessing existing work, and a blueprint for standard setting as the field converges on norms. Our framework extends prior work by centering evaluation on human performance rather than model output alone, formalizing causal inference through RCT methodology for AI contexts, integrating heterogeneity analysis and practical significance assessment, implementing a graded transparency and repeatability framework, and addressing AI-specific challenges including model versioning, human-AI interaction dynamics, contamination and spillover effects, and equitable impact assessment.
2605.02050.paper_summary_zh: 這項工作建立了一個基礎框架，用於標準化 AI 評估 RCT（有時稱為人類提升研究）。<lb/>我們借鑒來自具有成熟 RCT 傳統的學科中的實驗實踐，包括軟體工程、經濟學、臨床和健康科學以及心理學，採用 (Shadish et al., 2002) 的四項有效性框架，並在此基礎上擴展出第五項原則，即透明性、可重複性和驗證，這些原則是根據透明性和開放性促進指南 (TOP Guidelines) 進行調整的 (Center for Open Science, 2025)。<lb/>我們將所有五項原則具體化為 33 條針對 AI 評估 RCT 情境的指導方針，這些指導方針以要求、理由、實施說明和證據基礎的形式表達。<lb/>我們將這些原則和指導方針定位為 AI 評估 RCT 的三個關鍵角色：作為研究計劃的設計工具、評估現有工作的評估標準，以及隨著該領域朝著規範趨同而制定標準的藍圖。<lb/>我們的框架通過將評估重心放在人的表現上，而不僅僅是模型輸出，擴展了先前的工作，通過 RCT 方法論在 AI 情境中形式化因果推斷，整合異質性分析和實用意義評估，實施分級透明性和可重複性框架，並解決包括模型版本控制、人機互動動態、污染和溢出效應，以及公平影響評估等 AI 特定挑戰。
2605.02050.author: Christopher Kelly et.al.
2605.02050.authors: Christopher Kelly, Angelica Chowdhury, Alexandra Campili, Bimpe Ayoola, Devin Barbour, Thomas Chen Dawson, Ze Shen Chin, Rokas Gipiškis
